completed_tasks.name: Merge centered alignment defaults into standard
Merge prompt_align optional v1 extension into standard and JS reference engine

remaining_tasks.name: Compare actual Mainsail and Fluidd live append behavior and update protocol notes
Review frontend implementation implications for Fluidd, Mainsail, and KlipperScreen against current v1 spec

blockers.description: No technical blocker. External-repo posting or code changes require explicit user approval under the 3-confirmation rule.
blockers.workaround: Do read-only review of third-party repos unless the user explicitly confirms posting or changing code.

human_actions_pending.action: Provide explicit approval before any comments or code changes in repos not owned by the user.
human_actions_pending.context: The user clarified that the 3-time confirmation rule applies to posting anything in third-party repos.

decisions.decision: Default top-level prompt item alignment is centered.
decisions.rationale: Needed for portable prompt rendering across Fluidd, Mainsail, and KlipperScreen.

decisions.decision: prompt_align belongs in v1 as an optional extension, not future work.
decisions.rationale: Alignment is fundamental enough for macro authors and observed frontend behavior to standardize now.

decisions.decision: Normalized item output omits align for center and only emits left/right.
decisions.rationale: Keeps existing prompts and fixtures backwards-compatible while making non-default alignment explicit.

decisions.decision: prompt_input remains reserved future work, not v1.
decisions.rationale: Existing Mainsail experimental shape has unresolved lifecycle, escaping, validation, and multi-frontend issues.

next_action: Start by comparing Mainsail and Fluidd live append behavior, especially how each handles actions appended after prompt_show and whether content is live-rendered, buffered, ignored, or requires a new prompt.
context_notes: Repo main is current at d6cc0d9. All owned PRs are merged. Project goals remain wide support across Fluidd, Mainsail, and KlipperScreen plus backwards compatibility with existing prompt macros.
